data.content:
Implementation plan:
1. Restrict Claude managed-skill discovery to the project root only in `src/managed_skills.rs`. Remove the `HOME/.claude` candidate from `SkillTool::Claude::location_candidates`, update the Claude root hint to `./.claude`, and verify the shared `preferred_location` / `doctor_location` path continues to drive `kno skills install`, `uninstall`, `update`, `doctor`, and `doctor --fix` without any Claude user-scope fallback.
2. Update unit coverage in `src/managed_skills_tests.rs` to match the new contract. Remove test setup and assertions that create or expect `home/.claude`, add assertions that Claude exposes only the project location, and cover the negative case where Claude warns/errors if `./.claude` is absent even when `HOME` is set. Keep Codex and OpenCode user-level behavior unchanged.
3. Update CLI integration coverage in `tests/cli_skills.rs` so Claude flows validate only `./.claude/skills`. Rewrite the doctor/install scenarios to assert that reported paths and repairs are project-local, and drop any assumptions that Knots will inspect or clean user-level Claude state.
4. Update the managed-skills documentation in `README.md` to remove the Claude fallback wording and state clearly that Claude support is project-level only (`./.claude/skills`).
5. During implementation, run targeted tests first (`cargo test managed_skills`, `cargo test --test cli_skills`) and then `make sanity` before any push because this repo requires the full sanity gate.

Complexity: low to moderate; the behavior is centralized, but tests and docs must be updated consistently.
Risk: this is an intentional compatibility break for legacy `~/.claude/skills` users. After the change, Knots will no longer uninstall or repair those files; manual cleanup would be outside Knots, which matches the requested behavior.

Child knots: not needed; the change is small and cohesive.
